message:
🐛 Remove 4 duplicate missions (#1374)

- helm-9136 (workaround) — kept helm-12637 (actual fix PR #12879)
- install-kubeflow.json — kept platform-kubeflow.json (has commands)
- install-kyverno.json — kept platform-kyverno.json (has commands)
- install-metallb.json — kept platform-metallb.json (has commands)

Atlantis duplicates (#1019 + #1905) kept — different issues/PRs.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1,80 +0,0 @@
-{
-  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
-  "name": "helm-9136-nested-null-values-do-not-remove-keys-from-sub-charts",
-  "missionClass": "solution",
-  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
-  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
-  "mission": {
-    "title": "helm: Nested `null` values do not remove keys from sub-charts",
-    "description": "Nested `null` values do not remove keys from sub-charts. This issue affects 84+ users.",
-    "type": "troubleshoot",
-    "status": "completed",
-    "steps": [
-      {
-        "title": "Identify helm troubleshoot symptoms",
-        "description": "Check for the issue in your helm deployment:\n```bash\nhelm version\n```\nLook for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue."
-      },
-      {
-        "title": "Review helm configuration",
-        "description": "Inspect the relevant helm configuration:\n```bash\nhelm version\n```\n#2648 and #5184 allow for deletion of keys by setting null values in a values.yaml file."
-      },
-      {
-        "title": "Apply the fix for Nested `null` values do not remove keys from sub-charts",
-        "description": "For the general pattern like this, using `false` works, although Helm complains about replacing a table with a boolean. \n```\n      {{- if .Values.redis.securityContext }}\n      securityContext: {{- toYaml .Values.redis.securityContext | nindent 8 }}\n      {{- end }}\n```\n\nHelm says it \"cannot\"\n```yaml\nkibana:\n  resources:\n    requests:\n      cpu: 1000m\n      memory: 2Gi\n    limits:\n      cpu: null\n      memory: 2Gi\n```"
-      },
-      {
-        "title": "Confirm Nested `null` values do not remove keys from\u2026 is resolved",
-        "description": "Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:\n```bash\nhelm version\n```\nConfirm that the issue symptoms are gone."
-      }
-    ],
-    "resolution": {
-      "summary": "For the general pattern like this, using `false` works, although Helm complains about replacing a table with a boolean. \n```\n      {{- if .Values.redis.securityContext }}\n      securityContext: {{- toYaml .Values.redis.securityContext | nindent 8 }}\n      {{- end }}\n```\n\nHelm says it \"cannot\" replace the table, but ultimately does.\n\n> warning: cannot overwrite table with non table ...",
-      "codeSnippets": [
-        "kibana:\n  resources:\n    requests:\n      cpu: 1000m\n      memory: 2Gi\n    limits:\n      cpu: null\n      memory: 2Gi",
-        "apiVersion: v2\nname: elastic\ndescription: Elastic stack\ntype: application\nversion: 0.0.1\ndependencies:\n  - name: kibana\n    version: 7.9.3\n    repository: \"@elastic\"",
-        "...\nresources:\n  limits:\n    cpu: 1000m\n    memory: 2Gi\n  requests:\n    cpu: 1000m\n    memory: 2Gi"
-      ]
-    }
-  },
-  "metadata": {
-    "tags": [
-      "helm",
-      "graduated",
-      "app-definition",
-      "troubleshoot"
-    ],
-    "cncfProjects": [
-      "helm"
-    ],
-    "targetResourceKinds": [
-      "Deployment"
-    ],
-    "difficulty": "expert",
-    "issueTypes": [
-      "troubleshoot"
-    ],
-    "maturity": "graduated",
-    "sourceUrls": {
-      "issue": "https://github.com/helm/helm/issues/9136",
-      "repo": "https://github.com/helm/helm",
-      "pr": "https://github.com/helm/helm/pull/9138"
-    },
-    "reactions": 84,
-    "comments": 56,
-    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
-  },
-  "prerequisites": {
-    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
-    "tools": [
-      "helm",
-      "kubectl"
-    ],
-    "description": "A running Kubernetes cluster with helm installed or the issue environment reproducible."
-  },
-  "security": {
-    "scannedAt": "2026-03-13T06:20:16.943Z",
-    "scannerVersion": "cncf-gen-3.0.0",
-    "sanitized": true,
-    "findings": []
-  }
-}

@@ -1,145 +0,0 @@
-{
-  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
-  "name": "install-kubeflow",
-  "missionClass": "install",
-  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
-  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
-  "mission": {
-    "title": "Install and Configure Kubeflow on Kubernetes",
-    "description": "Kubeflow is a powerful Machine Learning Toolkit for Kubernetes that provides a modular and scalable platform for building AI applications. Installing Kubeflow allows you to leverage a comprehensive set of tools for the entire AI lifecycle, from model training to serving.",
-    "type": "deploy",
-    "status": "completed",
-    "steps": [
-      {
-        "title": "Add the Helm repository",
-        "description": "Add the Kubeflow Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.\n```bash\nhelm repo add kubeflow https://alauda.github.io/kubeflow-chart\nhelm repo update\n```"
-      },
-      {
-        "title": "Install Kubeflow using Helm",
-        "description": "Install Kubeflow in a dedicated namespace. This command will create the namespace if it doesn't exist.\n```bash\nhelm install kubeflow kubeflow/kubeflow --namespace kubeflow --create-namespace --version 1.6.2\n```"
-      },
-      {
-        "title": "Verify the installation",
-        "description": "Check that all pods are running in the Kubeflow namespace.\n```bash\nkubectl get pods --namespace kubeflow\n```"
-      },
-      {
-        "title": "Configure resource limits",
-        "description": "Set resource limits for the Kubeflow components by applying a custom configuration. Modify the `kubeflow-config.yaml` as needed and apply it.\n```bash\nkubectl apply -f kubeflow-config.yaml --namespace kubeflow\n```"
-      },
-      {
-        "title": "Access the Kubeflow dashboard",
-        "description": "Port-forward the Kubeflow dashboard service to access it locally.\n```bash\nkubectl port-forward svc/istio-ingressgateway -n kubeflow 8080:80\n```"
-      }
-    ],
-    "uninstall": [
-      {
-        "title": "Remove the Helm release",
-        "description": "Uninstall the Kubeflow release from your cluster.\n```bash\nhelm uninstall kubeflow --namespace kubeflow\n```"
-      },
-      {
-        "title": "Clean up CRDs and resources",
-        "description": "Delete any remaining Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) and other resources associated with Kubeflow.\n```bash\nkubectl delete crd $(kubectl get crd | grep kubeflow | awk '{print $1}')\n```"
-      },
-      {
-        "title": "Remove the namespace",
-        "description": "Delete the Kubeflow namespace to clean up any remaining resources.\n```bash\nkubectl delete namespace kubeflow\n```"
-      }
-    ],
-    "upgrade": [
-      {
-        "title": "Backup current state",
-        "description": "Before upgrading, back up your current Kubeflow configuration and resources.\n```bash\nkubectl get all --namespace kubeflow -o yaml > kubeflow-backup.yaml\n```"
-      },
-      {
-        "title": "Update the Helm repository",
-        "description": "Update the Helm repository to fetch the latest charts.\n```bash\nhelm repo update\n```"
-      },
-      {
-        "title": "Upgrade Kubeflow",
-        "description": "Upgrade your existing Kubeflow installation to the latest version.\n```bash\nhelm upgrade kubeflow kubeflow/kubeflow --namespace kubeflow --version 1.6.2\n```"
-      },
-      {
-        "title": "Verify the upgrade",
-        "description": "Check that all pods are running after the upgrade.\n```bash\nkubectl get pods --namespace kubeflow\n```"
-      }
-    ],
-    "troubleshooting": [
-      {
-        "title": "Pods stuck in Pending state",
-        "description": "If you see pods in a Pending state, check for resource availability in your cluster.\n```bash\nkubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace kubeflow\n```"
-      },
-      {
-        "title": "Service not reachable",
-        "description": "If the Kubeflow dashboard is not accessible, ensure that the port-forward command is running and check the service status.\n```bash\nkubectl get svc --namespace kubeflow\n```"
-      },
-      {
-        "title": "Helm release failed",
-        "description": "If the Helm installation fails, check the Helm release status for errors.\n```bash\nhelm status kubeflow --namespace kubeflow\n```"
-      },
-      {
-        "title": "Configuration changes not applied",
-        "description": "If changes to configurations are not reflected, ensure you applied the correct YAML file and check for errors in the logs.\n```bash\nkubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace kubeflow\n```"
-      }
-    ],
-    "resolution": {
-      "summary": "A successful installation of Kubeflow will have all components running in the 'kubeflow' namespace, and you will be able to access the Kubeflow dashboard via the specified port. Ensure that all pods are in the Running state.",
-      "codeSnippets": [
-        "helm repo add kubeflow https://alauda.github.io/kubeflow-chart\nhelm repo update",
-        "helm install kubeflow kubeflow/kubeflow --namespace kubeflow --create-namespace --version 1.6.2",
-        "kubectl get pods --namespace kubeflow",
-        "kubectl apply -f kubeflow-config.yaml --namespace kubeflow",
-        "kubectl port-forward svc/istio-ingressgateway -n kubeflow 8080:80"
-      ]
-    }
-  },
-  "metadata": {
-    "tags": [
-      "installation",
-      "configuration",
-      "cncf",
-      "app-definition",
-      "incubating"
-    ],
-    "cncfProjects": [
-      "kubeflow"
-    ],
-    "targetResourceKinds": [
-      "Service",
-      "Namespace",
-      "Ingress"
-    ],
-    "difficulty": "intermediate",
-    "issueTypes": [
-      "installation",
-      "configuration"
-    ],
-    "installMethods": [
-      "helm"
-    ],
-    "maturity": "incubating",
-    "projectVersion": "v1.10.0",
-    "containerImages": [
-      "registry/kubeflow/kubeflow:v1.10.0"
-    ],
-    "sourceUrls": {
-      "docs": "https://www.kubeflow.org/",
-      "repo": "https://github.com/kubeflow/kubeflow",
-      "helm": "https://alauda.github.io/kubeflow-chart"
-    },
-    "qualityScore": 100
-  },
-  "prerequisites": {
-    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
-    "tools": [
-      "helm",
-      "kubectl"
-    ],
-    "description": "Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and have installed Helm and kubectl on your local machine."
-  },
-  "security": {
-    "scannedAt": "2026-03-05T22:20:25.746Z",
-    "scannerVersion": "cncf-install-gen-1.0.0",
-    "sanitized": true,
-    "findings": []
-  }
-}

@@ -1,147 +0,0 @@
-{
-  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
-  "name": "install-kyverno",
-  "missionClass": "install",
-  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
-  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
-  "mission": {
-    "title": "Install and Configure Kyverno on Kubernetes",
-    "description": "Kyverno is a Kubernetes-native policy engine that enables security, compliance, automation, and governance through policy-as-code. Installing Kyverno allows you to enforce policies on your Kubernetes resources effectively.",
-    "type": "deploy",
-    "status": "completed",
-    "steps": [
-      {
-        "title": "Add the Kyverno Helm repository",
-        "description": "First, add the Kyverno Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration:\n```bash\nhelm repo add kyverno https://kyverno.github.io/kyverno\nhelm repo update\n```"
-      },
-      {
-        "title": "Install Kyverno using Helm",
-        "description": "Next, install Kyverno in your Kubernetes cluster with the following command. This will create the necessary namespace and install Kyverno v1.16.3:\n```bash\nhelm install kyverno kyverno/kyverno --namespace kyverno --create-namespace --version 3.6.3\n```\n\n**Note:** The chart version is `3.6.3` (app version v1.16.3). Do not use `--version v1.16.3` — that is the app version, not the chart version.\n\n**Version compatibility:** Chart `3.7.1` (v1.16.3) requires Kubernetes 1.32+ due to its dependency on the `MutatingAdmissionPolicy` v1alpha1 API. For Kubernetes 1.25–1.31 (including OpenShift 4.14–4.18), use chart `3.6.3` (v1.16.3).\n\n**OpenShift clusters:** Kyverno pods require Security Context Constraints (SCCs). After install, grant `nonroot-v2` SCC to all Kyverno service accounts:\n```bash\nfor sa in kyverno-admission-controller kyverno-background-controller kyverno-cleanup-controller kyverno-reports-controller; do\n  kubectl create clusterrolebinding \"kyverno-scc-${sa}\" --clusterrole=system:openshift:scc:nonroot-v2 --serviceaccount=\"kyverno:${sa}\"\ndone\nkubectl -n kyverno rollout restart deployment kyverno-admission-controller kyverno-background-controller kyverno-cleanup-controller kyverno-reports-controller\n```"
-      },
-      {
-        "title": "Verify the installation",
-        "description": "Check that the Kyverno pods are running successfully:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods --namespace kyverno\n```"
-      },
-      {
-        "title": "Configure Kyverno policies",
-        "description": "After installation, you can configure Kyverno policies. For example, to set the Pod Security Standard to 'baseline', you can modify the `values.yaml` file or set it directly in the Helm command:\n```bash\nhelm upgrade kyverno kyverno/kyverno --namespace kyverno --set podSecurityStandard=baseline\n```"
-      },
-      {
-        "title": "Test a sample policy",
-        "description": "To ensure Kyverno is working as expected, apply a sample audit policy. **Important:** Use `validationFailureAction: Audit` (not `Enforce`) to avoid breaking workloads during initial testing.\n\nCreate a YAML file named `sample-policy.yaml` and apply it:\n```yaml\napiVersion: kyverno.io/v1\nkind: ClusterPolicy\nmetadata:\n  name: require-labels\nspec:\n  validationFailureAction: Audit\n  rules:\n    - name: check-team-label\n      match:\n        any:\n        - resources:\n            kinds:\n            - Pod\n      validate:\n        message: \"The label 'team' is required.\"\n        pattern:\n          metadata:\n            labels:\n              team: \"?*\"\n```\nThen apply it:\n```bash\nkubectl apply -f sample-policy.yaml\nkubectl get clusterpolicy\n```"
-      }
-    ],
-    "resolution": {
-      "summary": "A successful installation of Kyverno will show the Kyverno pods in a running state when you execute `kubectl get pods --namespace kyverno`. You can also verify the configuration by applying a sample policy and checking for enforcement.",
-      "codeSnippets": [
-        "helm repo add kyverno https://kyverno.github.io/kyverno\nhelm repo update",
-        "helm install kyverno kyverno/kyverno --namespace kyverno --create-namespace --version 3.6.3",
-        "kubectl get pods --namespace kyverno",
-        "helm upgrade kyverno kyverno/kyverno --namespace kyverno --set podSecurityStandard=baseline",
-        "apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1\nkind: ClusterPolicy\nmetadata:\n  name: require-non-root\nspec:\n  validationFailureAction: enforce\n  rules:\n    - name: require-non-root\n      match:\n        resources:\n          kinds:\n            - Pod\n      validate:\n        message: \"Run as non-root user\"\n        pattern:\n          spec:\n            securityContext:\n              runAsUser: 1000"
-      ]
-    },
-    "uninstall": [
-      {
-        "title": "Remove the Helm release",
-        "description": "To cleanly remove the Kyverno installation, first uninstall the Helm release using the following command. This will remove the deployment and associated resources:\n```bash\nhelm uninstall kyverno --namespace kyverno\n```"
-      },
-      {
-        "title": "Clean up CRDs and persistent resources",
-        "description": "Next, remove any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) that were created during the installation. This ensures that no leftover resources remain:\n```bash\nkubectl delete crd clusterpolicies.kyverno.io\nkubectl delete crd policies.kyverno.io\n```"
-      },
-      {
-        "title": "Remove namespace and verify",
-        "description": "Finally, delete the Kyverno namespace to clean up any remaining resources. Verify that the namespace has been deleted:\n```bash\nkubectl delete namespace kyverno\nkubectl get namespaces\n```"
-      }
-    ],
-    "upgrade": [
-      {
-        "title": "Backup current state",
-        "description": "Before upgrading, it's a good practice to back up the current state of your Kyverno installation. You can do this by exporting the current configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all --namespace kyverno -o yaml > kyverno-backup.yaml\n```"
-      },
-      {
-        "title": "Update repo and run upgrade command",
-        "description": "Update the Helm repository to ensure you have the latest charts, then upgrade your Kyverno installation to the desired version:\n```bash\nhelm repo update\nhelm upgrade kyverno kyverno/kyverno --namespace kyverno --version v1.18.0\n```"
-      },
-      {
-        "title": "Verify the upgrade",
-        "description": "After upgrading, check that the Kyverno pods are running the new version successfully:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods --namespace kyverno\n```"
-      }
-    ],
-    "troubleshooting": [
-      {
-        "title": "Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff",
-        "description": "If you notice that Kyverno pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for more details on the failure:\n```bash\nkubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace kyverno\n```\nCommon issues include misconfigured policies or resource limits."
-      },
-      {
-        "title": "Kyverno policies not applying",
-        "description": "If your policies are not being enforced, verify that the policies are correctly applied and check the status:\n```bash\nkubectl get cpol --namespace kyverno\nkubectl describe cpol <policy-name> --namespace kyverno\n```"
-      },
-      {
-        "title": "Helm upgrade fails with version conflict",
-        "description": "If you encounter a version conflict during the upgrade, ensure that the version you are trying to upgrade to is compatible with your current installation. You can check the installed version with:\n```bash\nhelm list --namespace kyverno\n```"
-      },
-      {
-        "title": "Namespace not found error",
-        "description": "If you receive an error indicating that the namespace 'kyverno' is not found, ensure that the namespace exists and is correctly specified in your commands:\n```bash\nkubectl get namespaces\n```"
-      },
-      {
-        "title": "Admission controller TLS secret not created (K8s < 1.32)",
-        "description": "If the admission controller pod stays Not Ready with repeated `TLS handshake error: secret kyverno-svc.kyverno.svc.kyverno-tls-pair not found` messages, you are likely running Kyverno chart 3.7.1+ on Kubernetes < 1.32. Chart 3.7.1 (app v1.17.1) requires the `MutatingAdmissionPolicy` v1alpha1 API which is only available in Kubernetes 1.32+. The missing API blocks TLS certificate generation.\n\n**Fix:** Downgrade to chart 3.6.3 (app v1.16.3):\n```bash\nhelm uninstall kyverno --namespace kyverno --no-hooks\nkubectl -n kyverno delete secret -l owner=helm,name=kyverno\nhelm install kyverno kyverno/kyverno --namespace kyverno --create-namespace --version 3.6.3\n```"
-      },
-      {
-        "title": "Reports controller OOMKilled",
-        "description": "The Kyverno reports controller defaults to 128Mi memory limit, which may be insufficient for clusters with many workloads. If the pod shows OOMKilled, increase its memory:\n```bash\nkubectl -n kyverno patch deployment kyverno-reports-controller --type=json -p='[{\"op\":\"replace\",\"path\":\"/spec/template/spec/containers/0/resources/limits/memory\",\"value\":\"512Mi\"},{\"op\":\"replace\",\"path\":\"/spec/template/spec/containers/0/resources/requests/memory\",\"value\":\"256Mi\"}]'\n```"
-      }
-    ]
-  },
-  "metadata": {
-    "tags": [
-      "installation",
-      "configuration",
-      "cncf",
-      "security",
-      "incubating"
-    ],
-    "cncfProjects": [
-      "kyverno"
-    ],
-    "targetResourceKinds": [
-      "Namespace"
-    ],
-    "difficulty": "beginner",
-    "issueTypes": [
-      "installation",
-      "configuration"
-    ],
-    "installMethods": [
-      "helm"
-    ],
-    "maturity": "incubating",
-    "projectVersion": "v1.16.3",
-    "containerImages": [
-      "ghcr.io/kyverno/kyverno:v1.16.3"
-    ],
-    "sourceUrls": {
-      "docs": "https://kyverno.io",
-      "repo": "https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno",
-      "helm": "https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno/tree/main/charts/kyverno-policies/"
-    },
-    "qualityScore": 100
-  },
-  "prerequisites": {
-    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
-    "tools": [
-      "helm",
-      "kubectl"
-    ],
-    "description": "Ensure you have a running Kubernetes cluster and the Helm CLI installed."
-  },
-  "security": {
-    "scannedAt": "2026-03-01T01:09:32.532Z",
-    "scannerVersion": "cncf-install-gen-1.0.0",
-    "sanitized": true,
-    "findings": []
-  }
-}

@@ -1,141 +0,0 @@
-{
-  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
-  "name": "install-metallb",
-  "missionClass": "install",
-  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
-  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
-  "mission": {
-    "title": "Install and Configure Metallb on Kubernetes",
-    "description": "MetalLB is a network load-balancer implementation for bare metal Kubernetes clusters, utilizing standard routing protocols. Installing MetalLB allows you to expose services externally in a Kubernetes environment that does not have a cloud provider's load balancer.",
-    "type": "deploy",
-    "status": "completed",
-    "steps": [
-      {
-        "title": "Add the MetalLB Helm repository",
-        "description": "Add the official MetalLB Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration with the following command:\n```bash\nhelm repo add metallb https://metallb.github.io/metallb\nhelm repo update\n```"
-      },
-      {
-        "title": "Install MetalLB using Helm",
-        "description": "Install MetalLB in the `metallb-system` namespace with the specified version:\n```bash\nhelm install metallb metallb/metallb --namespace metallb-system --create-namespace --version v0.15.3\n```"
-      },
-      {
-        "title": "Verify the installation",
-        "description": "Check that the MetalLB pods are running successfully:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods --namespace metallb-system\n```"
-      },
-      {
-        "title": "Configure MetalLB with IP Address Pool",
-        "description": "Create a ConfigMap to define the IP address pool for MetalLB. Replace `<YOUR_IP_RANGE>` with your desired IP range:\n```bash\ncat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -\napiVersion: v1\nkind: ConfigMap\nmetadata:\n  namespace: metallb-system\n  name: config\ndata:\n  config: |\n    address-pools:\n    - name: default\n      protocol: layer2\n      addresses:\n      - <YOUR_IP_RANGE>\nEOF\n```"
-      },
-      {
-        "title": "Check MetalLB service",
-        "description": "Ensure that MetalLB is properly configured and can allocate IPs by checking the service:\n```bash\nkubectl get svc --namespace metallb-system\n```"
-      }
-    ],
-    "resolution": {
-      "summary": "A successful installation of MetalLB will show the MetalLB pods running in the `metallb-system` namespace, and you will be able to configure IP address pools for load balancing. You can verify the service allocation by checking the services in the namespace.",
-      "codeSnippets": [
-        "helm repo add metallb https://metallb.github.io/metallb\nhelm repo update",
-        "helm install metallb metallb/metallb --namespace metallb-system --create-namespace --version v0.15.3",
-        "kubectl get pods --namespace metallb-system",
-        "cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -\napiVersion: v1\nkind: ConfigMap\nmetadata:\n  namespace: metallb-system\n  name: config\ndata:\n  config: |\n    address-pools:\n    - name: default\n      protocol: layer2\n      addresses:\n      - <YOUR_IP_RANGE>\nEOF",
-        "kubectl get svc --namespace metallb-system"
-      ]
-    },
-    "uninstall": [
-      {
-        "title": "Remove the Helm release",
-        "description": "Uninstall the MetalLB release from the `metallb-system` namespace using Helm to remove all associated resources.\n```bash\nhelm uninstall metallb --namespace metallb-system\n```"
-      },
-      {
-        "title": "Clean up CRDs and persistent resources",
-        "description": "Delete the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) associated with MetalLB to ensure no lingering resources remain.\n```bash\nkubectl delete crd configmaps.metallb.io\nkubectl delete crd addresspools.metallb.io\n```"
-      },
-      {
-        "title": "Remove namespace and verify",
-        "description": "Delete the `metallb-system` namespace and verify that it has been removed successfully.\n```bash\nkubectl delete namespace metallb-system\nkubectl get namespaces\n```"
-      }
-    ],
-    "upgrade": [
-      {
-        "title": "Backup current state",
-        "description": "Before upgrading, back up the current configuration of MetalLB by exporting the ConfigMap.\n```bash\nkubectl get configmap config -n metallb-system -o yaml > metallb-config-backup.yaml\n```"
-      },
-      {
-        "title": "Update repo and run upgrade command",
-        "description": "Update the Helm repository and upgrade MetalLB to the latest version. Ensure you specify the desired version.\n```bash\nhelm repo update\nhelm upgrade metallb metallb/metallb --namespace metallb-system --version v0.15.4\n```"
-      },
-      {
-        "title": "Verify the upgrade",
-        "description": "Check that the MetalLB pods are running the new version and are healthy after the upgrade.\n```bash\nkubectl get pods --namespace metallb-system\n```"
-      }
-    ],
-    "troubleshooting": [
-      {
-        "title": "Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff",
-        "description": "If MetalLB pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for errors that may indicate configuration issues.\n```bash\nkubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace metallb-system\n```"
-      },
-      {
-        "title": "IP address not allocated",
-        "description": "If MetalLB is not allocating IP addresses, verify that the ConfigMap is correctly configured and that the IP range is valid.\n```bash\nkubectl describe configmap config -n metallb-system\n```"
-      },
-      {
-        "title": "Service not reachable",
-        "description": "If services exposed by MetalLB are not reachable, check the service configuration and ensure that the MetalLB pods are running.\n```bash\nkubectl get svc --namespace metallb-system\nkubectl get pods --namespace metallb-system\n```"
-      },
-      {
-        "title": "MetalLB not responding to ARP requests",
-        "description": "If MetalLB is not responding to ARP requests, ensure that your network configuration allows ARP broadcasts and that MetalLB is configured correctly.\n```bash\nkubectl get pods --namespace metallb-system -o wide\n```"
-      }
-    ]
